124113.explanation: Although it is a noble goal, we believe quality must be decisive.

124114.explanation: DOS supports more extensive participation opportunities. We see great value in resident panels and early information provision.

124117.explanation: Although we always approach such initiatives with a 'Yes, unless' attitude (meaning: it must be possible unless there are compelling reasons against it), we cannot pre-determine that everything is always appropriate.

124118.explanation: Sustainability subsidies are an important part of achieving the goals that we as a municipality have.

124119.explanation: We review all initiatives for business activities against the established frameworks.

124125.explanation: This way residents can still enjoy fireworks; it might be wise to organize it in combination with associations/organizations from the districts.

124128.explanation: Inclusivity is highly valued by DOS. If we can encourage entrepreneurs and associations to become more inclusive through targeted subsidies, we should not neglect that.

124129.explanation: DOS believes that if the set conditions and the housing care vision are met, building on agricultural land should be allowed.

124130.explanation: In our program, we have proposed membership discounts for every new member. This way we keep our residents vital and our associations vibrant.

124132.explanation: This is a spearhead of DOS. Following the Integral Education Accommodation Plan initiated by us, a substantial number of tasks have been identified. We want to continue addressing these diligently!

124136.explanation: In recent years, we have seen the opposite trend and welcome it. More and more mini-libraries and school libraries bring books close to residents again. Accessibility is very important.

124138.explanation: DOS is in principle in favor of more camera surveillance in places where frequent misconduct occurs. However, we emphasize that we do not want to infringe on residents' privacy if it is not necessary.

124139.explanation: Women’s shelters (“Stay away from my body houses”) must be located in places where people, including politicians, know as little as possible about them. This is to protect victims as much as possible.

124143.explanation: As long as holiday homes comply with all laws and regulations.

124144.explanation: We are not inherently against expansion of the districts. As shown with Lindenhagen, this can work well in a limited number of places on the edges of existing districts.

124145.explanation: Although this is a noble aim, we have noticed in the past that it costs the municipality more money to provide this than the society structurally yields.

124147.explanation: This threshold currently, at 130%, is already above the national average.

124149.explanation: We have policies for this and want to continue them. The need is leading.

124150.explanation: This is already policy; the car is a guest in the neighborhood.

124151.explanation: With the shortages we experience in the healthcare sector, we cannot afford the luxury of choices based on religion or culture. Besides, the municipality does not have authority over this.

124157.explanation: We continue to meet the objectives imposed on us by the national government. It is positive that there are resources available to achieve this.

124158.explanation: For the vast majority of waste streams, we certainly agree. However, there will always be some streams that require a small fee; the polluter pays.

124159.explanation: No black meadows! In our program, we mainly propose looking at possibilities on buildings.

124160.explanation: Steinerbos is a basic facility and should therefore also be supported by the municipality as such.

124164.explanation: This depends on the situation. If by renting out rooms we can make a property attractive for some young starters, this can be valuable for the municipality. We support creative solutions to the housing shortage.

124165.explanation: If we may interpret village center as a meeting place, then this fits seamlessly with what we have in our program and on which our core policies are built.

124166.explanation: The center of Stein is currently full, but optimization of parking facilities can be considered.

124168.explanation: We have long been striving for compensation for victims from those responsible for the problems. So Rijkswaterstaat is responsible for this.

124170.explanation: Currently, after research, there is no need for a separate senior advisory council. The Social Domain advisory council places great importance on the interests of seniors.

124113.explanation: CMB prioritizes suitability and experience over gender. Mandatory equal division of men and women can result in not necessarily appointing the most qualified candidates, but gender becoming a decisive factor. Our council deserves the best qualified candidate.

124114.explanation: CMB is a strong advocate for participation through referenda. By allowing our residents to directly participate in decisions on important local issues, municipal decisions gain more support and people feel more heard.

124117.explanation: CMB supports the preservation of the countryside, employment, and a vital local economy. Farming businesses should be given this space, provided that neighbors are informed at an early stage and can actually influence the plans, for example, through consultation moments.

124118.explanation: There are plans for a South Limburg heating network using residual heat from Chemelot as a source. But what happens if companies there stop? The chemical sector faces changes. CMB does not want to wait for these developments and prefers investing in sustainable energy at home.

124119.explanation: CMB opposes the arrival of distribution centers in the municipality of Stein. With Chemelot, the A2 highway, and the asphalt plant in our backyard, we as a municipality already bear more than enough environmentally burdensome activities and nuisance from odor, light, and noise for our residents.

124125.explanation: CMB is in favor. A nationwide fireworks ban will take effect from the next turn of the year. Celebrating New Year's Eve with fireworks is a deep-rooted tradition. That tradition must be honored, and therefore the municipality should take the lead.

124128.explanation: CMB chooses to spend public money on public (municipal) areas and facilities. Investing in accessibility is part of good and future-proof entrepreneurship, and it is not reasonable to use public funds for modifications to private properties.

124129.explanation: CMB favors infill development and opposes expansion. Housing construction should take place within the town center and not come at the expense of green land.

124130.explanation: CMB supports continuing Stein’s participation in the Sport & Culture Limburg fund. This fund supports, among other things, on behalf of the municipality of Stein, contributions for people living around the subsistence minimum.

124132.explanation: CMB is supportive but notes that work is often not completed within budget. It is painful to waste public money on temporary emergency buildings for schools. CMB advocates for more realistic budgeting.

124136.explanation: Every core village must have strong facilities for sports, culture, library, and community rooms. In Stein, we have one large library operated by De Domijnen. Nevertheless, every village can have an 'open bookshelf' that takes over the role of traditional libraries.

124138.explanation: Addressing nuisance and crime requires tailored approaches. CMB opts for a area-focused approach where police, youth work, and the municipality collaborate structurally, and where camera surveillance is not shunned as a means to increase the chance of catching offenders.

124139.explanation: There are shelter locations in the Westelijke Mijnstreek region. Currently, these partners have not requested expanding shelter facilities in Stein.

124143.explanation: Stein suffers from a tight housing market. To counter this shortage and prevent vacancies, such measures are more than welcome.

124144.explanation: CMB supports infill development, not expansion. That is, building new homes or buildings within the existing built-up area, rather than extending into the outskirts. This way, we preserve our valuable greenery.

124145.explanation: CMB advocates introducing an obligation to perform community service in Stein. Those who receive social assistance benefits from the municipality must provide a counter-performance—unpaid work that benefits society.

124147.explanation: The municipality does not need to raise the income threshold for poverty assistance further because it is already set at 120% of the minimum income, offering a generous safety net without unnecessarily straining municipal resources.

124149.explanation: CMB supports this. The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars to encourage sustainable transport, facilitate the use of electric vehicles, and contribute to cleaner air and a better climate.

124150.explanation: CMB opposes this. The maximum speed within built-up areas should not be lowered to 30 km/h everywhere because this limit already applies on almost all roads, and the few roads still at 50 km/h are necessary for smooth traffic flow. These roads are also traffic-safe at 50 km/h.

124151.explanation: CMB opposes residents always being able to choose a healthcare provider based on religion, culture, or beliefs because the municipality, when using public funds, must focus on quality and price, not personal convictions.

124157.explanation: CMB is against priority for status holders in social housing. Our own residents come first.

124158.explanation: CMB will not abolish waste disposal fees. These are cost-covering in Stein, meaning this tax is only used to cover the costs of waste processing. However, CMB supports free disposal of household waste at the recycling center 2 to 4 times per year.

124159.explanation: CMB focuses on energy transition with social support. There is no support for solar parks but there is for insulation, solar panels on roofs, and home batteries. CMB therefore aims for subsidized self-sustainability, where the municipality itself must set a good example.

124160.explanation: Steinerbos must remain, but its financial situation cannot continue as is due to (including municipal) mismanagement. CMB calls for a fundamentally different approach: split the park into independent sections, make the park area freely accessible, and ensure sustainable operation.

124164.explanation: The municipality should not prohibit landlords from renting out individual rooms because this contributes to affordable housing and offers flexibility for students, starters, and low-income people.

124165.explanation: Optimal safety and strong facilities in every village (sports, culture, meeting places) are essential conditions. Every initiative that promotes meeting and increases livability is important. Village centers must remain or return in every village, as in Meers.

124166.explanation: CMB aims for a different traffic and parking situation around the shopping center/Moutheuvel by implementing mandatory one-way parking spaces and limiting their availability.

124168.explanation: No additional financial compensation is necessary for Stein residents due to the closure of the Urmond bridge because the municipality has already allocated substantial funds for emergency measures and traffic safety. Thanks to CMB, this amount has been increased to 2.5 million euros.

124170.explanation: Elderly policy receives attention, including from other parties. In our view, the crowning achievement should be establishing an elderly advisory council. This still does not exist. The alternative is “Seniors in charge,” an initiative by KBO Limburg also embraced by the Stein branch.

124113.explanation: Equal treatment of women and men. Also in governance.

124114.explanation: Further develop in the coming council term the conditions this must meet within the framework of mutual expectations.

124117.explanation: Small hospitality businesses/campsites add value to the municipality and residents.

124119.explanation: Must fit within municipal boundaries/environmental factors.

124129.explanation: Sharing existing homes and allowing care residences.

124136.explanation: Already provided for at school communities.

124144.explanation: Should actually expand within existing regulations.

124147.explanation: Has already been raised and must be adjusted regionally otherwise.

124149.explanation: If there is a shortage, yes.

124160.explanation: Basic social provision within our municipality.

124164.explanation: But keeping it within acceptable limits for the neighborhood.

124168.explanation: Already carried out, but only for residents and associations within the Oud Urmond area.

124113.explanation: It is good to strive for a proportional distribution, but it is about the complementarity of the executive board and the right man or woman in the right place.

124114.explanation: Council members are elected to make decisions on behalf of residents. They weigh interests, have information and expertise, and make integrated choices within legal frameworks, for which they are accountable through the democratic process.

124117.explanation: Agriculture must become significantly more sustainable, but we also want farmers to be able to earn money from their business. A shop with good products from their own land can help with that. A campsite can cause nuisance, so this can only be done in consultation with the surroundings. We want to enable these options.

124118.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

124119.explanation: Space is limited in Stein. A new distribution center would not use space efficiently. Moreover, they spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid migrant laborers. As far as we are concerned, the arrival of more distribution centers must stop.

124125.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

124128.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

124129.explanation: Housing is scarce, also here in Stein. An extra home on the farmyard can be a good solution, either as an additional source of income or as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We want to make this possible for farmers who want it.

124130.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

124132.explanation: A good school requires a good building. It is the municipality’s responsibility to provide school buildings where safe and pleasant learning is possible. Investing in that is investing in our future.

124136.explanation: There is already a full-fledged library in Stein with a complete range of services. We do want lending points in every large village as well.

124138.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not think more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a great good that should not be infringed lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the streets who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

124139.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. Stein is too small for that, but we do support such a joint initiative in the Western Mining Region.

124143.explanation: We have no holiday homes in Stein, and we do not want them either. Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage.

124144.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the green areas around our villages as well as possible. But to address the housing shortage, we also need to use agricultural land for housing. That is also an opportunity to create pleasant, green neighborhoods for our residents.

124145.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

124147.explanation: We want to raise the income threshold for poverty assistance to 130% of the social assistance standard. Silent poverty also exists, including in Stein. Therefore, we want to raise the income threshold to 130% so that more people can receive help from the municipality.

124149.explanation: There are increasingly more electric cars. The municipality must facilitate this as well.

124150.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

124151.explanation: There are few people who can provide WMO household help. We do have many people who need this care. Therefore, it is undesirable that people can refuse assistance.

124157.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are not the only group given priority for social housing. They are one of several vulnerable groups that we must support and provide with a roof over their heads.

124158.explanation: Litter on the streets is annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. It is much better if people bring their waste to the recycling center so that the municipality can properly separate and process it. This results in clean streets and a healthy environment. Therefore, we want to make the recycling center in Stein free for private individuals.

124159.explanation: Space in Stein is limited. The land can be better used for housing or nature.

124160.explanation: We want the Steinerbos to remain accessible and affordable for residents. As the sole shareholder, the municipality has a facilitating role. Because resources are limited, it may be necessary in the long term to consider an adjusted business model.

124164.explanation: We have too few suitable homes, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented at a fair price.

124165.explanation: It is not possible to realize something in every village.

124166.explanation: There are already many parking spaces in a small area. More space should be made for greenery in the center of Stein, not more paving.

124168.explanation: An amount of 2.5 million euros has been allocated by the entire council to find a solution for the Urmond bridge. This money is for the community but not for individual residents.

124170.explanation: The elderly in Stein already have a voice through KBO Limburg. This organization has the group 'seniors, taking action themselves' and many topics are also linked to the social domain advisory council. The advisory council has vacancies, seniors can join, and they can start a new seniors working group.

124113.explanation: The VVD Stein promotes diversity but rejects mandatory gender ratios. Governance should be done with the best people, not with imposed numbers.

124114.explanation: Residents of Stein must certainly have influence on important topics, but not through binding or structural referendums. VVD Stein chooses a strong municipal council, clear responsibility, and serious prior participation.

124117.explanation: Yes, unless. VVD wants to give farmers room to do business but opposes a generic 'always allow' approach. Customized solutions and clear rules ensure that economic freedom does not come at the expense of livability and legal equality.

124118.explanation: VVD Stein supports local, sustainable energy initiatives but believes that municipal financial support should not be automatic. The municipality facilitates where possible, encourages where necessary, and invests only temporarily and purposefully when demonstrably in the public interest.

124119.explanation: VVD opposes unnuanced opposition to new distribution centers. Instead, it advocates clear frameworks, spatial management, and market forces, where good initiatives are given space and bad plans are rejected.

124125.explanation: VVD Stein is not a principled supporter of municipal fireworks displays but is pragmatically open to tailored solutions if these demonstrably contribute to safety and livability, without unnecessary taxpayer burdens and with respect for individual freedom.

124128.explanation: Accessibility is important, but not an automatic subsidy task of the municipality. VVD chooses entrepreneurship, customized solutions, and a government that supports where needed but does not structurally co-pay for private investments.

124129.explanation: It should be possible for farmers to build houses on their land themselves, provided this is done carefully, within municipal control, and with an eye for livability and affordability. This way, VVD combines solving the housing shortage with room for entrepreneurship and respect for ownership.

124130.explanation: VVD opposes structural and automatic payment of sports club memberships. However, we support targeted, temporary, and effective support where it demonstrably contributes to participation, health, or opportunities for children, within clear frameworks.

124132.explanation: VVD Stein chooses quality over quantity, focused investment where it is really needed, with respect for affordability and responsibility. This fits a government that handles residents’ money carefully. The integrally established housing plan by the council is leading in this.

124136.explanation: There is currently only one library in Stein.

124138.explanation: Where camera surveillance helps make Stein safer, it should be deployed. Where other measures are more effective, those deserve preference. Safety is a core government task but always balanced with freedom and responsibility.

124139.explanation: VVD Stein firmly supports the protection of domestic violence victims. A shelter can be a necessary last-resort facility for people in acute danger.

124143.explanation: VVD chooses order, legal certainty, and economic vitality. No generic legalization of permanent residence in holiday homes, but pragmatic customized measures where demonstrably sensible and necessary.

124144.explanation: Housing is not an ideological issue but a practical problem requiring flexible solutions. This is in the interest of starters, families, and the elderly. From this perspective, VVD’s position fits choice, growth, and realism.

124145.explanation: For VVD Stein: rights and duties should be balanced. Activation should prevail over passivity. Customized solutions instead of one-size-fits-all. This way, social assistance remains a solid safety net, while everyone who can participates to their capacity.

124147.explanation: VVD opposes automatic increases in income thresholds. Where bottlenecks arise, the municipality must solve them with targeted customization, not by structurally widening the safety net. That is socially and financially responsible.

124149.explanation: More parking spaces can be designated as charging spots, provided this is demand-driven, does not worsen parking pressure, and the market is used optimally. VVD chooses realism over dogma and smart growth instead of obligation.

124150.explanation: Traffic measures must be effective, proportional, and locally customized, not generic bans or obligations without clear added value.

124151.explanation: Maximum freedom of choice where this is practical and effective.

124157.explanation: Housing allocation must be fair, transparent, and as much as possible based on objective criteria such as waiting time and urgency due to medical or social circumstances. Additionally, integration is better promoted through customization and distribution rather than automatic priority for scarce social rental housing.

124158.explanation: A (limited) financial incentive raises cost awareness and prevents excessive or unnecessary waste disposal. Municipalities can work more efficiently and invest in better service and sustainability without increasing local charges.

124159.explanation: VVD chooses municipal customization: first solar panels on roofs, infrastructure, and business parks, but no ideological ban on agricultural land. Local considerations, with attention to landscape, are preferred over rigid regulations.

124160.explanation: The Steinerbos swimming pool fulfills an important social function. VVD therefore chooses a businesslike and realistic approach, working towards a future-proof operation with as little structural subsidy as possible.

124164.explanation: Not banning, but regulating where necessary. The government must set conditions and enforce them, not unnecessarily intervene in the market or limit property rights.

124165.explanation: VVD Stein chooses flexibility, realism, and local initiatives. Village centers where they work and are desired: yes. A mandatory village center for every village: no.

124166.explanation: More parking spaces may be needed, but always as part of a broader vision for an entrepreneurial, accessible, and attractive center of Stein.

124168.explanation: VVD says no to general financial compensation but yes to smart, targeted solutions for those demonstrably disproportionately affected. Improving accessibility and reopening the bridge as soon as possible remain the best forms of compensation.

124170.explanation: Policy must be made integrally and consider all residents of Stein, young and old. VVD therefore chooses efficient, low-threshold participation within existing frameworks instead of new consultation structures.

statements.title: There must be financial compensation for the residents of Stein because the Urmond bridge will be closed for 18 months.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Stein.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The residents of Stein must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Stein moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Stein must have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Stein should have one larger library instead of smaller libraries in each village.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There must be more parking spaces in the center of Stein.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must stop giving money to the Steinerbos swimming pool.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Stein must have an advisory council for seniors, possibly together with other municipalities.
